mentions.note: Eric's portfolio; own it don't trade it; consistent strong buy.
Down 20% year-to-date after poor quarter and baby formula lawsuit loss; questions whether belongs on 52-week low list; needs couple reasons on call to go higher.
Jennifer's portfolio; retail but also lot of tech; amazing conglomerate; keep in portfolio for tech/retail blend.
Will be solid; prefer solid over nothing; Wednesday earnings with Morgan Stanley.
Loved by Wall Street; estimates always too low; stock jumps predictably like 'trained rabbit'; likely to jump higher next week on earnings.
Nico's portfolio; cheap gas not great for them but high gas drives card signups which is profit driver; rolling out more gasoline stations.
Nico's portfolio; Cramer doubts Mark Benioff's data center story; software long knives out; will be hard own.
Legacy stock from caller's deceased father; amazingly company; comparable to NewCore; hold as tribute and quality.

Interview with CEO Michael Intrader on Squawk on Street; won huge order from Anthropic; Cramer pressed on profitability path, Intrader candid (could be profitable in 3 months but opportunity too large); transparency rewarded with 11% stock gain.
Caller Roz throws chair; Cramer misjudged it, down and flat past months but still profitable; stocks seem gutsy to like but still does; would buy more here.

Caller Sean asking about offshore drilling super cycle; Cramer says no, just go with Chevron (CEO Mike Worth drills offshore best); Caller Jim asks Exxon vs Chevron; Cramer sticks with Chevron—Worth runs better company.
Broke out 20 points; copper exposure in diversified portfolio; commodity/mining theme.
Huge believer 2026 will be consolidation year; big banks will buy regional banks under looser regulatory regime; Fifth Third is candidate; would really buy it.
Data center energy turbines company; Cramer wants exposure in portfolio; suggested swap for Sony but alternative consideration; powers nat gas facilities for data centers.
Eric's portfolio; crushing it; data center, YouTube, everywhere; one of absolute favorite holdings.

Cramer's alma mater with big position in travel trust; well-positioned to profit from volatile markets through risk management; expects solid Q1 numbers and good reaction barring Iranian action weekend.
Regional bank in Johnny's portfolio; consolidation candidate under looser regulatory regime; M&A upside in 2026.
Best companies in truck industry that nobody listens to except Cramer; great conference call; after three down years industry finally on fire; stock headed higher.

Owned in travel trust; company transformed shedding slower-growing divisions, focusing on research for life-saving drugs; most blockbusters and best pipeline in industry; often hammered on news release then rallies on conference call.
Terrific bank but many important inputs to quarter; CEO loves caution so Wall Street finds quibbles even if unwarranted; earnings day Tuesday with Wells and Citi.

Scaled via acquisitions into top-tier defense; operates in communications, space, electronic warfare, ISR; owns Aerojet Rocketdyne (missile propulsion) acquired ~3 years ago—increasingly valuable as U.S. rebuilds missile stockpile; counter-drone products (Vampire System) in high demand.

Behind THAAD (Terminal High Altitude Area Defense) and Aegis BMD systems for ballistic missile defense; sells missile systems and prior-generation fighter jets to allies; massive missile replenishment cycle supports multi-year run.
Eric's portfolio; Cramer would swap for J&J; thinks J&J will have better growth; healthcare exposure but J&J superior.

CEO Brendan Foley interview: Cramer gave free pass on giant Unilever acquisition (Hellman's mayo/brands); didn't press on cost despite skepticism; in retrospect should have asked tougher questions about execution vs. Unilever.
Eric's portfolio; networking chip company; tech/AI infrastructure play.
Should have good quarter; expect fabulous 2026 given robust IPO pipeline for second half; traders fired up instrumental in good quarterly results.

Jennifer's portfolio: too close to SAP; both struggling; doing terribly trading-wise (companies okay fundamentally but stock action poor); Cramer recommends swap out for J&J and RTX to preserve capital.
Jennifer's portfolio; entertainment company; keep as part of media/entertainment diversification.
Juggernaut; came out of nowhere to build greatest entertainment company on earth; broke up with Warner Bros but collected $2.8B breakup fee; can keep doing what doing; give benefit of doubt.

CNBC Investing Club wrong on Nike; Piper downgrade today cited lack of innovation under returning CEO Elliott Hill; company has more problems than realized; turn difficult; Cramer been wrong not early; gave until October (2 years in) but admits error in judgment.
Portfolio comment from Johnny: 'finally breaking out great'; growth engine in diversified portfolio; consistent Cramer favorite.

Caller Jerry bought in 170s, looking to add on decline; Cramer says very tough to value, plunged into data center build at questionable time; don't like what developing away from Oracle; prefers to see June quarter first; wants to preserve capital.

Second best packaged food company in world (after Coca-Cola); impressed by CEO Raml Magguire's navigation of processed food travails, GLP-1 weight loss drugs, healthy diet trends; demonstrates listening to customer.

Trump endorsed on True Social citing war-fighting capabilities; software analytics firm combining multi-source data for Pentagon insights; down nearly 40% from October highs due to AI displacement fears (misplaced); Trump endorsement may help; rebuilding operations intelligence critical for defense.
Regional bank consolidation candidate; one of three good ones (Fifth Third, Regions, Trusts) likely acquisition targets as big banks seek consolidation under looser regulatory regime.
Eric's portfolio; maybe best defense company on Earth; replaces Medtronic in Cramer's suggested rebalance; missile/defense exposure critical.

Behind Patriot missiles (gold standard), SM3 interceptors, Tomahawk cruise missiles; government low on Tomahawks after recent Iran strikes; benefits from hot commercial aerospace market; up 58% last year; maybe best defense company on Earth.
Jennifer's portfolio: too close to Microsoft; both struggling; trading terribly; Cramer recommends swap out along with Microsoft for J&J and RTX.
Fundamental change at company under new CEO Rob Lynch (winner); previous management didn't run company well; Cramer call bullish despite past losses.
Jennifer's portfolio; movies/entertainment; Cramer considered swapping for GE Vernova (data center energy) but ultimately kept Sony for entertainment exposure.
Nico's portfolio; automation theme; great tremendous theme; warehouse automation play.
Regional bank consolidation candidate; one of three good ones likely acquisition targets as big banks seek consolidation under looser regulatory regime.
Cramer is 'an Uber guy'; loves Trist; thinks very very cheap right here; implied contrast to Lyft which caller sold at top.
Nico's portfolio; heavy dividend; Cramer not as convinced need yield, prefers growth but willing to go along if caller convinced.
Owned in travel trust; long-term turnaround story under CEO Charlie Sharpe who's willing to buy back stock aggressively; not an earnings story but fundamental transformation play.
Incredible run; consolidating terrifically; retail exposure consolidating well; liked in diversified portfolio.
